title: conyza vernonioides subsp. arborea
intro: Conyza vernonioides subsp. arborea, also known as tall fleabane, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to various parts of the world, including Australia and New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The tall fleabane plant has been known to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing skin irritations, making it a valuable natural remedy. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its leaves and stems can also be used in poultices and compresses to speed up the healing process.
